title: croton mucronifolius
intro: Croton mucronifolius, also known as the Nepal croton, is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family, Euphorbiaceae. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of this plant have been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Croton mucronifolius include reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial and fungal infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
